Enum Class Grid.ValueFormat

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<Grid.ValueFormat>
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.Grid.ValueFormat
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<Grid.ValueFormat>, Constable
Enclosing class:
Grid
public static enum Grid.ValueFormat extends Enum<Grid.ValueFormat>
Defines the different formats used to represent a cell's location on the grid.

To balance performance, memory usage, and readability, the solver uses multiple ways to identify a cell. This enum provides a type-safe way to distinguish between them and prevents the use of "magic numbers," improving maintainability.

Since:
2025.07 - ValueFormat Enum Introduction
Performance Characteristics:
O(1) access time.
Threading Model:
Thread-safe as an immutable enum.
Memory Management:
Minimal memory overhead as a singleton per enum constant.

  • Enum Constant Summary

    Enum Constants
    Enum Constant
    Description
    Bitmask
    A format representing the entire grid state as a bitmask.
    Index
    A zero-based index from 0 to 108, representing a cell's position in the flattened grid.
    PackedInt
    A human-readable format where a cell's location is encoded as (row * 100 + col).

  • Enum Constant Details

    • PackedInt

      public static final Grid.ValueFormat PackedInt
      A human-readable format where a cell's location is encoded as (row * 100 + col).

      For example, the cell at row 3, column 5 is represented as 305. This format is intuitive and simplifies adjacency arithmetic, but it is not as memory-efficient as Index. It is primarily used for debugging, configuration, and initial adjacency calculations during static initialization. While cell identifiers are stored as shorts, the "PackedInt" name is retained for historical consistency.

      Since:
      2025.07 - ValueFormat Enum Introduction
      See Also:
      • Grid.indexToPacked(short)
      • Grid.packedToIndex(short)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as an immutable enum.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory overhead as a singleton per enum constant.

    • Index

      public static final Grid.ValueFormat Index
      A zero-based index from 0 to 108, representing a cell's position in the flattened grid.

      This is the primary format used in many performance-critical code paths, such as in caches and generator tasks. It offers a compact and efficient way to iterate over cells and is the native format for most internal data structures (along with Bitmask). However, it is less intuitive for humans to read compared to PackedInt.

      Since:
      2025.07 - ValueFormat Enum Introduction
      See Also:
      • Grid.indexToPacked(short)
      • Grid.packedToIndex(short)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as an immutable enum.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory overhead as a singleton per enum constant.

    • Bitmask

      public static final Grid.ValueFormat Bitmask
      A format representing the entire grid state as a bitmask. This format is not used to identify individual cells but is included for completeness.

      The grid state is stored internally as a long[2] bitmask. This enum value signifies operations or data related to this internal representation, such as the Grid.click(long[]) method. Methods that accept or return a single cell value will typically throw an IllegalArgumentException if this format is specified.

      Since:
      2025.07 - ValueFormat Enum Introduction
      See Also:
      • Grid.gridState
      • Grid.clearBit(int)
      • Grid.click(long[])
      • Grid.getBit(int)
      • Grid.getGridState()
      • Grid.setBit(int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as an immutable enum.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory overhead as a singleton per enum constant.

    • valueOf

      public static Grid.ValueFormat valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
